title: ampelocissus nervosa
intro: Ampelocissus nervosa, also known as the Indian grapevine, is a type of climbing plant native to tropical Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Ampelocissus nervosa include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are often made into teas or infusions, and its extracts are used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health issues.
